20-plus-years: More than two decades shipping narrative, marketing, and code.
about-closing-cta: Want to know more about me? Keep reading or let's talk.
about-how-i-build-body: I architect, design, and develop the product. AI assists me in stacking the bricks. Orchestration through Claude and Codex on Node, Python… or whatever framework each deploy needs: vanilla, Astro, Flask, React; static or serverless as required. CSP-enforced, hash-pinned, with high-performance budgets in CI. The stack stays boring on purpose; the work above it… most interesting.
about-how-i-work-body: Remote, async, end-to-end ownership. No throw-it-over-the-wall handoffs, no half-built scopes, no specs that die in production. Surfaces sometimes small, sometimes not so small, but always defensible, documented, tested, with CI deploy gates as a recurring part of the work.
about-what-i-do-body: I ship systems to production. Right now: Scanner 21.719, which audits Chilean websites against Ley 21.719; multi-tenant SaaS like AGLAYA Kanban Desk; AI-driven email marketing automation infrastructure built to scale; and websites with the most solid architecture.
about-who-i-am-body: Ibai Fernández. AI Product Engineer. Spaniard, EU citizen, remote from Belo Horizonte. I run AGLAYA — The Uncomfortable Agenc·AI, where I craft systems that free entrepreneurs from traditional SaaS lock-in and turn their investments into long-lasting assets. I move between narrative, marketing, and engineering; most of what I take on today demands all three at once.
acquire-expertise: Added a structured product and problem-framing layer through design thinking and human-centered innovation methods.
acquired-valuable: Cross-cultural communication training that strengthened international collaboration and market adaptation.
advanced-expertise: Advanced work in language, pedagogy, and cognition that later informed UX writing, messaging, and narrative framing.
alfredo-recommendation: Ibai is a person with a high level of involvement in his projects, constantly seeking to understand how things around him work, which means he continues to acquire new skills. Without a doubt, he will overcome any challenge that comes his way.
as-ops-director: Founded and directed a 100% remote digital innovation agency, operating as the lead systemic integrator for B2B operations. I engineered digital growth architectures by fusing Marketing Intelligence, the Lean Canvas Business Model, and Design Thinking methodologies.
bani-recommendation: Ibai Fernández is a recent and invaluable asset to our team. His unique blend of skills in audiovisuals, development, and marketing automation is rare. Despite running a successful business, he finds time for complex requests. His peers at LFi Agency describe him as joyful, eloquent, kind, and direct. His ability to overdeliver and provide a wider perspective has lightened our workload. His sense of humor and commitment to timely solutions make him an integral part of our team. I wholeheartedly recommend Ibai for his consistent delivery of solutions that hugely contribute to our success. Ibai rocks! 🤘
brands-that-believe-in-me: Brands and teams I've shipped for
building-and-maintaining-strong-relation: Translating business needs into execution plans, priorities, and realistic delivery decisions.
career-path-h2: Two decades, four companies, from cinema to AI.
cecilia-recommendation: Ibai is a proactive person who meets the set objectives and adapts easily to work teams. He constantly seeks to improve himself and creates an excellent work environment.
cookie-consent-body: This site uses Google Analytics 4 to understand how visitors arrive and what they read — nothing else, nothing sold, nothing shared. Decide what you prefer. Either way, you'll still see the site; only your analytics signal differs.
crafting-attractive-functional-responsiv: Building public-facing sites, landing flows, dossiers, and product surfaces that are fast, maintainable, and ready to ship.
crafting-digital-marketing-strategies: Connecting traffic, messaging, UX, and analytics so acquisition work improves through actual evidence instead of guesswork.
crafting-visually-captivating-intuitivel: Clarifying offers, journeys, and interfaces so the story, the screen, and the next action support each other.
crafting-visually-engaging-email-campaig: Designing email programs, segmentation logic, reporting loops, and operational workflows for teams that need consistency at scale.
creation-of-engaging-high-quality-multim: Developing copy, messaging structures, and multimedia assets that make complex work easier to understand and easier to trust.
designing-and-delivering-professional-de: Helping teams or user bases understand new tools, workflows, and digital processes so rollout actually sticks.
developing-managing-budgets-resources: Engineered and deployed proprietary real-world eCommerce and retail laboratories to relentlessly test and validate UX/UI, digital acquisition, and automated customer retention hypotheses.
diploma-at-msu: Diploma in Media Management & Production (ISEP Scholar)
diploma-in-design-thinking: Certification in Design Thinking: Strategy for Innovation
elm-st-fna-closing: This foundational decade forged my "unfair advantage" as a Systemic Integrator: the deep understanding that before you can automate a digital conversion ecosystem, you must first master the human narrative that drives it.
elm-st-fna-deployment-1: Narrative Semiotics & Behavioral Triggers: Wrote, directed, and produced fiction short films and multimedia projects, engineering deep psychological triggers that I now apply to high-converting digital user journeys.
elm-st-fna-deployment-2: End-to-End Creative Governance: Commanded the entire lifecycle of digital and audiovisual products, from initial requirement gathering and conceptualization to final deployment and market distribution.
elm-st-fna-deployment-3: Early Digital Integration: Architected comprehensive brand identities and early digital acquisition ecosystems, seamlessly blending SEO, web design, and digital advertising with high-end audiovisual production.
elm-st-fna-intro: Founded and directed an independent creative laboratory that served as my ultimate training ground for human psychology and visual semiotics. Long before writing code or automating CRMs, I mastered the psychological flow of the message through cinematic direction and scriptwriting.

exp-aglaya-2-r1-resp-1: Algorithmic Sovereignty — autonomous corporate cognition, owned by the client, not the vendor.
exp-aglaya-2-r1-resp-2: Inevitable Logical Systems — design systems whose progression is logical, not reactive, so growth compounds rather than rebuilds.
exp-aglaya-2-r1-resp-3: Zero-Filter Diagnostics — eliminate the market noise and the operational fog so the command picture is honest.

exp-elm-st-r1-closing: Storytelling, semiotics, and media production — capabilities that later translated directly into digital systems.
exp-elm-st-r1-intro: Founded an audiovisual production company at the intersection of cinematic craft and brand storytelling. Built the narrative foundation that informed much of the digital work that followed from 2016 onwards. It allowed me to start my next professional step as Creative Director at NIC.ec, where I later moved into Marketing.
exp-elm-st-r1-resp-1: Operated the studio end-to-end: pre-production, shoot, post, delivery.
exp-elm-st-r1-resp-2: Brand films, music videos, corporate documentaries for SMB + agency clients.
exp-elm-st-r1-resp-3: Cross-border operation: seeded in Springfield, Missouri; founded in Málaga (Spain); expanded into Ecuador after moving there in 2014.
exp-lfi-meta: Enterprise CRM & Marketing Automation · Chilean Company, Remote from Colombia · 3 Roles in 3 Years… and Counting
exp-lfi-r1-intro: Joined LFi as Mail Marketing and Automation Specialist for enterprise CRM campaigns. Shipped responsive email templates and customer-facing surfaces against deliverability and accessibility constraints.
exp-lfi-r1-resp-1: HTML/CSS templates for transactional and commercial email at enterprise scale.
exp-lfi-r1-resp-2: QA and cross-client rendering against the major MTAs and webmail clients.
exp-lfi-r1-resp-3: Pair-programmed with the automation team on dynamic content blocks.
exp-lfi-r2-intro: Moved into the Marketing Coordinator role. Coordinated the content, design, and automation tracks across multiple enterprise accounts.
exp-lfi-r2-resp-1: Account briefing, creative kickoffs, and delivery oversight.
exp-lfi-r2-resp-2: QA gate before client preview and sign-off cycles.
exp-lfi-r2-resp-3: Onboarded new accounts into the LFi operating playbook.
exp-lfi-r3-closing: My mandate: apply a product mindset to email — building automation MVPs to validate marketing hypotheses fast, then promoting them to hard code for scalable execution.
exp-lfi-r3-intro: I don't just manage lists or campaigns; I design data architectures that automate the relationship between enterprise brands and users at a global scale. As Director of Automation, I'm building the traditional email-marketing function into a strict discipline of revenue engineering.
exp-lfi-r3-resp-1: AI-Driven Orchestration: LLMs and autonomous agents generating dynamic, hyper-personalized content in real time, cutting deployment cycles from days to a couple of hours.
exp-lfi-r3-resp-2: Full-Stack Architecture: Custom integrations with Next.js, TypeScript, and Python bridging complex CRMs and high-volume delivery engines.
exp-lfi-r3-resp-3: Predictive Behavioral Logic: Parametric user journeys, decision trees, and behavioral triggers for 18+ enterprise accounts across Banking, Energy, and Retail.
exp-lfi-r3-resp-4: Data-Driven Deliverability: Dynamic content injected at scale plus relentless audit of server deliverability and CTR/ROI optimization.

exp-nic-ec-r1-resp-1: Brand identity redesign and visual system.
exp-nic-ec-r1-resp-3: Creative direction across web, print, traditional and digital AV media, and event surfaces.
exp-nic-ec-r1-resp-4: Hired and led a small in-house design team.

exp-nic-ec-r2-resp-3: Coordinated creative, marketing, and sales as one go-to-market unit.

exp-nic-ec-r3-resp-2: Channel mix optimization, attribution model, CAC/LTV reporting.
exp-nic-ec-r3-resp-3: Stakeholder coordination across legal, ops, and IT.
exploring-my-journey-across-continents: The map keeps changing. The delivery doesn't.
fernanda-recommendation: Ibai is a great professional and person. He cares deeply about his team and the work, constantly seeks ways to optimize tasks, and has broad expertise in email marketing. It is a pleasure to work with him day to day!
focused-on-seo: Deepened hands-on work in SEO, SEM, paid media, and acquisition strategy for measurable digital growth.
franco-recommendation: Ibai is a true digital alchemist, turning ideas into effective and memorable email marketing campaigns. He has a level of care for detail that makes the difference, delivering impeccable campaigns that generate results and reflect his passion for what he does.
gained-comprehensive: This is where the narrative base was built: audiovisual storytelling, semiotics, and media production before those skills were translated into digital systems.

lfi-atlas-bani-quote: His ability to overdeliver and provide a wider perspective has lightened our workload. He brings structure, speed, and clarity even under pressure.
lfi-atlas-final-copy: That is the commercial point of the LFi dossier. I did not just ship assets inside an agency. I helped engineer a more governable delivery machine: clearer routing, cleaner qualification, faster launches, stronger QA, and better trust under enterprise pressure.
lfi-atlas-final-kpi-2: Next modules: Banco Internacional + Bill Capital
lfi-atlas-final-proof-1: Lead quality and routing can be made inspectable instead of improvised.
lfi-atlas-final-proof-2: Deliverability debt is operational debt, not a cosmetic email problem.
lfi-atlas-final-proof-3: More revenue confidence came from systems thinking, not louder output.
lfi-atlas-final-title: THE VALUE WAS NEVER "MORE OUTPUT." IT WAS MAKING DELIVERY HARDER TO BREAK.
lfi-atlas-franco-quote: Ibai is a true digital alchemist, transforming ideas into effective and memorable email marketing campaigns. His attention to detail makes a measurable difference in both execution quality and outcomes.
lfi-atlas-hero-title: WHEN AN AGENCY STOPS BLEEDING TIME, MARGIN APPEARS.
lfi-atlas-lead: At LFi, my most valuable work was not another campaign asset. It was engineering the operating layer that made delivery harder to break.
lfi-atlas-leben-context-1: Within my coordination scope at LFi, the Leben intervention combined technical reputation recovery, list hygiene, ESP migration, and conversion-focused email UX.
lfi-atlas-leben-context-2: The important point is not authorship theatre. It is that the repair followed an explicit operating sequence and turned a noisy, risky base into a healthier commercial channel.
lfi-atlas-leben-delivery-rate-copy: after blacklist recovery, warm-up, SPF, DKIM, and DMARC
lfi-atlas-leben-hard-bounces-copy: 99.8% reduction after cleanup and infrastructure hardening
lfi-atlas-leben-kpi-2: Mailchimp to Brevo migration plus mobile-first redesign
lfi-atlas-leben-sequence-intro: Inside the area I coordinated, the Leben repair followed four operating moves before the performance lift became visible in the numbers.
lfi-atlas-leben-snapshot-desc: Chilean real-estate brand at ileben.cl whose email operation had accumulated deliverability debt, list noise, and weak conversion performance despite a large contact base.
lfi-atlas-leben-step-1-copy: Blacklist recovery, IP warm-up, and SPF, DKIM, and DMARC hardened the infrastructure before scale resumed.
lfi-atlas-leben-step-2-copy: SQL cleanup, bounce removal, and activity filters reduced a noisy 69k-contact base into a list the channel could actually serve.
lfi-atlas-leben-step-2-title: Cut the base to a usable core
lfi-atlas-leben-step-3-copy: The Mailchimp to Brevo migration lowered fragility and gave the team a cleaner, more governable operating surface.
lfi-atlas-leben-step-4-copy: Mobile-first email UX, WhatsApp entry points, and send-time learning made the channel commercially useful again.
lfi-atlas-leben-title: LEBEN SHOWED THAT DELIVERABILITY IS A REVENUE PROBLEM.
lfi-atlas-norden-change-1: B2C and B2B stopped being treated as one email program.
lfi-atlas-norden-change-2: The visible reset began on a depurated active base instead of a wide, indiscriminate blast surface.
lfi-atlas-norden-change-3: WhatsApp exits, Norden landings, and UTMs stayed attached to the channel as standard operating discipline.
lfi-atlas-norden-change-4: Segmentation became a decision layer, not a decorative filter applied after the send.
lfi-atlas-norden-context: What matters here is not yet the later intelligence workspace. It is the client-side improvement: the channel became easier to govern, more precise, and measurably healthier on the live account itself.
lfi-atlas-norden-fact-2-value: blast logic with mixed B2C/B2B pressure
lfi-atlas-norden-fact-3-value: 7,116 sends at reset, ~9,418 active B2C base later
lfi-atlas-norden-intro: Inside my LFi scope, the Norden shift began in August 2025 by replacing indiscriminate sending with a more segmented operating model tied to active-base control, standardized tracking, and cleaner audience logic.
lfi-atlas-norden-kpi-1: August 2025 visible reset under LFi ownership
lfi-atlas-norden-kpi-2: Segmented B2C/B2B operation instead of indiscriminate sending
lfi-atlas-norden-kpi-3: Tracking plus WhatsApp plus landing discipline held at account level
lfi-atlas-norden-sequence-intro: The Norden shift did not start with prettier campaigns. It started by resetting the live base, separating audience logic, and making the send layer more governable before asking the account for performance.
lfi-atlas-norden-snapshot-desc: Dental health client whose email account had visible volume, but still behaved too much like a blast machine, with mixed audience logic and too little operational separation between B2C and B2B.
lfi-atlas-norden-step-1-copy: The visible reset began on a depurated active segment, not on the old wide blast surface that mixed too many audiences into one send logic.
lfi-atlas-norden-step-2-copy: The channel stopped treating patient communication and agreement-based outreach as if they were the same program with the same commercial rhythm.
lfi-atlas-norden-step-3-copy: WhatsApp exits, Norden landings, and UTM discipline stayed attached to the channel so the account could be read and improved coherently.
lfi-atlas-norden-step-4-copy: Performance stopped rewarding list breadth alone and started favoring audience logic that proved measurably healthier than wide-list blasts.
lfi-atlas-norden-title: NORDEN IMPROVED WHEN SEGMENTATION BECAME OPERATING DISCIPLINE.
lfi-atlas-pablo-quote: Ibai goes forward and does not stop until he becomes a specialist. He seeks the reasons behind things, always goes the extra mile, and brings the kind of teamwork and learning velocity you want near high-pressure systems.
lfi-atlas-page-title: LFi: Agency Operating Systems | Project Dossier
lfi-atlas-pocuro-change-1: Meta-heavy acquisition was rebalanced toward higher-intent search demand.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-change-2: WhatsApp chatbot flows absorbed repetitive qualification work before human follow-up.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-change-3: Lead scoring created a visible split between raw capture and real buyer readiness.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-change-4: Sales stopped drowning in volume and started receiving structured, better-prioritized opportunities.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-context-1: Within my LFi scope, POCURO was generating more than 60,000 monthly leads from Meta and Google Ads, but fewer than 5% showed real purchase intent. The problem was not demand. It was an acquisition machine optimized for quantity that the sales team could not process efficiently.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-context-2: That made the job concrete: reduce low-value interactions, rebalance spend toward higher-intent channels, automate first-line qualification, and hand sales a structured view of buyer intent instead of raw noise.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-funnel-step-1: raw monthly demand entering the acquisition layer
lfi-atlas-pocuro-funnel-step-2: manageable qualified pool after automation and scoring
lfi-atlas-pocuro-funnel-step-3: high-intent buyers routed toward sales each month
lfi-atlas-pocuro-kpi-3: Sales team focused on signal, not noise
lfi-atlas-pocuro-sequence-intro: The POCURO intervention did not begin with a prettier funnel. It began by separating raw acquisition volume from sales-ready demand and making that difference operationally visible.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-snapshot-desc: Chilean real-estate developer whose acquisition engine had been optimized for volume, not for buyer readiness or sales usability.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-step-1-copy: Channel strategy moved away from brute-force volume once higher-intent search demand proved commercially healthier than Meta-heavy capture alone.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-step-2-copy: A WhatsApp chatbot built with n8n and a custom knowledge base absorbed the repetitive filtering work before a human sales touch was required.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-step-3-copy: Lead scoring created a visible split between raw demand, manageable qualified pool, and buyers who actually deserved downstream attention.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-step-3-title: Score intent instead of guessing it
lfi-atlas-pocuro-step-4-copy: The result was not just fewer leads. It was structured, prioritized handoff that let the sales layer work on signal instead of noise.
lfi-atlas-pocuro-title: POCURO DIDN'T NEED MORE LEADS. IT NEEDED A BETTER FILTER.
lfi-atlas-proof-intro: This was not a single-account sandbox. The operating layer had to sustain banking, real estate, energy, health, and institutional workloads without collapsing back into improvisation.
lfi-atlas-proof-signal-1: Selected proof modules published now: POCURO, Leben, and Norden.
lfi-atlas-proof-signal-2: Client breadth demonstrates load, not vanity.
lfi-atlas-proof-signal-3: Testimonials show the operator behind the systems while the client wall shows the pressure around them.
lfi-atlas-proof-title: THE CLIENT WALL MATTERS BECAUSE THE PRESSURE WAS REAL.
lfi-atlas-protocol: LFi operating atlas // revenue operations & automation
lfi-atlas-summary-1: I entered through execution, but the leverage emerged when I turned tacit workflow knowledge into reusable systems: CRM routing logic, launch blueprints, quality gates, escalation paths, multichannel orchestration, and documentation that reduced dependency on individual memory.
lfi-atlas-summary-2: This dossier is about commercial reliability. Not "marketing output" in the abstract, but the systems thinking that made delivery faster, clearer, and more governable under real enterprise pressure.
lfi-atlas-systems-cell-1-copy: I turned lead movement into a governed layer. Qualification, ownership, escalation, and downstream actions stopped depending on informal tribal knowledge.
lfi-atlas-systems-cell-2-copy: Recurring delivery work became a system of repeatable patterns rather than a weekly reconstruction exercise. That is where cycle compression came from.
lfi-atlas-systems-cell-3-copy: I made quality less dependent on heroics by introducing pre-flight checks, decision criteria, and escalation routes that kept execution legible under pressure.
lfi-atlas-systems-cell-4-copy: Process knowledge was written down and turned into reusable assets so the team could scale capability without scaling fragility.
lfi-atlas-systems-intro: The common failure mode inside agencies is not lack of effort. It is hidden operating debt: routing logic spread across heads, launches rebuilt from scratch, QA depending on mood, and account complexity turning every campaign into a fresh firefight.
lfi-atlas-systems-signal-1: Documented process to reduce dependence on specific operators.
lfi-atlas-systems-signal-2: CRM and lead routing logic made explicit and inspectable.
lfi-atlas-systems-signal-3: Reusable launch structures for regulated and multi-sector accounts.
lfi-atlas-systems-title: THE AGENCY DID NOT NEED MORE OUTPUT. IT NEEDED AN OPERATING LAYER.
lfi-atlas-timeline-intro-1: My progression inside LFi was not a sequence of prettier job names. It followed a simple logic: each role gave me more control over the underlying machine.
lfi-atlas-timeline-intro-2: That same systems bias also fed internal entrepreneurship inside LFi. Projects like MyBoard, reporteria-mm-a, and automation-tools were conceived to create leverage for the agency, not to compete with the client-delivery work that produced them.
lfi-atlas-timeline-role-1-copy: I entered through execution: front-end surfaces, email marketing, automation workflows, and the first routing patterns that removed repetitive setup work from the team.
lfi-atlas-timeline-role-2-copy: Scope widened into multichannel ecosystem control, funnel analytics, team coordination, and execution governance across simultaneous client operations.
lfi-atlas-timeline-role-3-copy: I took ownership of a broader operational layer: process documentation, automation standards, cost control across provider accounts, and a more predictable delivery model under regional enterprise pressure.
lfi-atlas-timeline-title: TITLE ONLY MATTERS WHEN IT TRACKS WIDENING SYSTEM OWNERSHIP.
lfi-fea-closing: This role served as the crucible for integrating Front-End development with advanced behavioral marketing, proving that clean code and systemic logic directly correlate with exponential business growth.
lfi-fea-core-deployment-1: Coded and deployed highly optimized, responsive Front-End architectures and dynamic email ecosystems focused on maximizing user retention and Click-Through Rates.
lfi-fea-core-deployment-2: Bridged the gap between technical development and behavioral marketing, translating raw business objectives into automated, scalable digital workflows.
lfi-fea-intro: Engineered high-conversion digital interfaces and robust marketing automation ecosystems for the Chilean and LatAm markets. I focused on transitioning traditional web design into predictive, data-driven revenue engines.
lfi-fea-pocuro-achievement-1: Architected and programmed complex, automated decision trees to classify massive databases, radically accelerating the acquisition of high-value real estate prospects.
lfi-fea-pocuro-achievement-2: Engineered advanced Lead Scoring algorithms and dynamic re-marketing workflows that skyrocketed lead generation and delivered a substantial ROI on marketing automation.
lfi-fea-pocuro-achievement-3: Optimized internal campaign pipelines by conducting precise data segmentation and automating repetitive lead-classification tasks, significantly increasing overall deployment efficiency.
lfi-mad-closing: My mandate is to apply a "Vibe Coding" product mindset: building automation MVPs to rapidly validate marketing hypotheses, and using hard code as a tool for massive scalability rather than mere execution.
lfi-mad-intro: I don't just manage lists or campaigns; I design data architectures that automate the relationship between enterprise brands and users at a global scale. As Director of Automation, I have evolved the traditional email marketing function into a strict discipline of revenue engineering.
lfi-mad-responsibility-1: AI-Driven Orchestration: Leveraging LLMs and autonomous agents to generate dynamic, hyper-personalized content in real-time, drastically reducing deployment cycles from weeks to mere hours.
lfi-mad-responsibility-2: Full-Stack Architecture: Developing custom integrations using Next.js, TypeScript, and Python to bridge the gap between complex CRMs and high-volume delivery engines.
lfi-mad-responsibility-3: Predictive Behavioral Logic: Coding parametric user journeys, automated decision trees, and behavioral triggers for 18+ major corporate accounts across the Banking, Energy, and Retail sectors.
lfi-mad-responsibility-4: Data-Driven Deliverability: Injecting dynamic content at scale while relentlessly auditing server deliverability protocols and performance metrics to maximize Click-Through Rates and direct ROI.
lfi-mc-closing: I utilized this role not merely to execute strategies, but to actively engineer the bridge between tactical marketing operations and massive systemic automation—directly catalyzing my subsequent elevation to Automation Director.
lfi-mc-deployment-1: Strategic Multichannel Orchestration: Directed sophisticated advertising and email workflows, implementing advanced lead segmentation and behavioral automation to exponentially increase user conversions.
lfi-mc-deployment-2: Deep Funnel Analytics: Executed relentless performance analysis and continuous workflow optimization, systematically maximizing the operational efficiency and ROI of all digital acquisition channels.
lfi-mc-deployment-3: Interdisciplinary Integration: Aligned web development, UX/UI design, and narrative communication teams to architect seamless, high-converting digital experiences.
lfi-mc-intro: Promoted to take command of multichannel advertising ecosystems and the deep analysis of complex conversion funnels. In this pivotal role, I shifted the agency's operational focus from traditional campaign management to high-impact, data-driven systemic growth.

nic-ec-mcd-closing: This executive role solidified my capability to govern digital ecosystems at a national scale, seamlessly integrating systemic team leadership with massive technological adoption.
nic-ec-mcd-deployment-1: Mass-Scale Digital Governance: Engineered comprehensive brand strategies and communication plans to drive the expansion and retention of a portfolio exceeding 45,000 active domains, aligning creative assets with aggressive national sales objectives.
nic-ec-mcd-deployment-2: Tech Evangelism & Narrative Positioning: Authored high-stakes corporate messaging and spearheaded nationwide digital literacy initiatives, successfully training and onboarding 6,600+ businesses into e-commerce and digital conversion ecosystems.
nic-ec-mcd-deployment-3: Cross-Functional Team Leadership: Directed and scaled a multidisciplinary department of 10 marketing and creative professionals, ensuring the flawless execution of high-impact institutional campaigns and market-facing positioning.
nic-ec-mcd-intro: Commanded the strategic marketing and corporate communications operations for the official administrator of Ecuador's top-level domain (.ec). I architected high-level brand strategies and multichannel communication frameworks to govern a massive digital infrastructure of over 45,000 active national domains.
nic-ec-mcm-closing: This operational leadership role forged my ability to turn raw market data into scalable campaign architectures, mastering the operational agility required to govern massive marketing deployments.
nic-ec-mcm-deployment-1: Cross-Functional Orchestration: Bridged the gap between product management, sales, and executive leadership, engineering cohesive go-to-market strategies that aligned diverse departmental objectives into a single revenue engine.
nic-ec-mcm-deployment-2: Data-Driven Campaign Deployment: Directed the end-to-end execution of multichannel advertising, leveraging deep data analytics to continuously audit performance, optimize workflows, and maximize targeted brand exposure.
nic-ec-mcm-deployment-3: Market Intelligence & Viability: Spearheaded advanced market research initiatives to rigorously validate the commercial viability of new digital services, ensuring narrative precision to attract and retain high-value customer segments.
nic-ec-mcm-deployment-4: Agency & Resource Governance: Governed internal creative teams and external media agencies, optimizing resource allocation and guaranteeing the flawless execution of complex deliverables under strict deadlines.
nic-ec-mcm-intro: Orchestrated the tactical execution of national marketing initiatives and cross-functional operations. I was tasked with translating high-level corporate vision into data-driven digital campaigns, ensuring precise market alignment and aggressive brand expansion.
norden-registry-boundary-intro: The page has to sell the way Ibai works, not the brand where the architecture happened to be audited most deeply. That means diagnosis first, build second, and clean autonomy at the end.
norden-registry-boundary-title: DIAGNOSE THE REALITY. BUILD THE SYSTEM. LEAVE IT WORKING.
norden-registry-chain-intro: The tool only deserves to exist if its chain is explicit from diagnosis to safe delivery. The filenames matter less than the fact that the logic stays inspectable from start to finish.
norden-registry-chain-step-1-copy: Every build starts by pulling Brevo into dated local state so evidence stops living inside ephemeral platform memory.
norden-registry-chain-step-2-copy: Each build fixes voice, stakeholder rules, intervention anchors, and attribution boundaries before interpretation begins.
norden-registry-chain-step-3-copy: Openers, clickers, overlap, recurrence, and operational grouping are derived locally so decisions stop depending on screenshots and memory.
norden-registry-chain-step-4-copy: The same base is compiled into internal reading, stakeholder reporting, printable output, and documentation instead of forking into separate stories.
norden-registry-chain-step-5-copy: What goes public is always a sanitized bundle, never the raw app, the private layer, or the career extraction that supports positioning.
norden-registry-chain-title: THE SYSTEM EARNS ITS PLACE WHEN THE CHAIN FROM DIAGNOSIS TO OUTPUT STAYS LEGIBLE.
norden-registry-claim-1: Ibai can diagnose when a business problem is really a systems problem in disguise.
norden-registry-claim-2: He can build local-first tools that turn platform chaos into readable, stakeholder-safe, decision-grade structure.
norden-registry-claim-3: He can hand those tools off in a way that reduces dependence instead of creating more of it.
norden-registry-deployment-1-copy: Useful knowledge gets trapped inside exports, UI fragments, provider quirks, and human memory, so nobody can really inspect what is going on.
norden-registry-deployment-1-title: The operation becomes unreadable beyond dashboards and screenshots
norden-registry-deployment-2-copy: Once every audience gets its own manual report, truth fragments. The system exists to keep one evidence base alive across multiple safe readings.
norden-registry-deployment-2-title: Internal reading, stakeholder reporting, and public proof start saying different things
norden-registry-deployment-3-copy: Without a proper system, handoff quality collapses. The build has to leave documentation, outputs, and a stakeholder-safe layer that survive after the builder steps out.
norden-registry-deployment-3-title: The knowledge depends on the original operator staying in the loop forever
norden-registry-family-intro: The tool only matters if the problem it solves is clear. This page exists because Brevo knowledge repeatedly breaks in the same three places.
norden-registry-family-title: THE TOOL EXISTS TO FIX OPACITY, DRIFT, AND OPERATOR DEPENDENCE.
norden-registry-final-future: The right public reading is not “nice dashboard” or “big client list.” It is “solution designer”: someone who can audit reality, architect the right tool, and leave behind a system that still works after the build is over.
norden-registry-final-title: THIS PAGE SELLS A WAY OF WORKING: DIAGNOSE CLEARLY, BUILD DELIBERATELY, AND LEAVE THE SYSTEM WORKING.
norden-registry-hero-note: The professional signal is not “nice report” or “big client logo.” It is “I can design and ship the infrastructure that makes opaque operations readable, transferable, and safer to run.”
norden-registry-hero-title: WHEN BREVO BECOMES OPAQUE, BUILD THE SYSTEM THAT MAKES IT LEGIBLE.
norden-registry-intro-1: The interesting claim here is not where the architecture first became robust. The interesting claim is that when a platform becomes unreadable through screenshots, exports, memory, and ad hoc commentary, Ibai can design and build the tool that restores legibility.
norden-registry-intro-2: What matters publicly is the ability to diagnose the problem, build the system, and leave behind a stakeholder-safe, operator-safe, handoff-ready layer that can keep working without narrative drift.
norden-registry-lead: This page is not about a client name. It is about a system built to diagnose opaque Brevo operations and turn them into readable, governable decision surfaces.
norden-registry-lfi-dossier-point-1: The first job is to expose what is actually broken: opacity, drift, unsafe reporting, and hidden dependence.
norden-registry-lfi-dossier-point-2: That means asking the uncomfortable questions, mapping the surfaces, and refusing decorative analytics.
norden-registry-lfi-dossier-point-3: The system is a response to diagnosed reality, not a toy looking for a problem.
norden-registry-lfi-dossier-title: Reality audit before any tool gets built
norden-registry-manifest-outputs-value: internal / stakeholder-safe / printable / docs / public bundle
norden-registry-manifest-rule-value: one evidence base / multiple safe readings / zero public drift
norden-registry-metric-1-note: the system prevents reporting drift by compiling from one audited layer
norden-registry-metric-3-note: snapshots, cohorts, and reporting stay inspectable outside fragile SaaS memory
norden-registry-metric-4-note: delivery is designed for clean handoff, not permanent builder dependence
norden-registry-norden-dossier-point-1: The build turns platform output into snapshots, cohorts, readable surfaces, and safe publication paths.
norden-registry-norden-dossier-point-2: The handoff layer matters just as much: documentation, printable outputs, stakeholder-safe bundles, and zero dependence on hidden context.
norden-registry-norden-dossier-point-3: That is the actual offer signal: build a tool, transfer sovereignty, and leave the system working.
norden-registry-norden-dossier-title: A local-first tool plus an operator-safe handoff layer
norden-registry-proof-1-copy: Instead of accepting “the operation feels messy,” the tool forces the mess into inspectable layers, boundaries, and decisions.
norden-registry-proof-1-title: It turns vague operational pain into a concrete systems problem
norden-registry-proof-2-copy: Cohorts, overlap, views, and reporting surfaces stop being scattered workarounds and become one designed system.
norden-registry-proof-2-title: It converts platform behavior into reusable operating structure
norden-registry-proof-3-copy: The system does not end as a clever internal layer. It ends as something that can be handed over, circulated, and operated safely.
norden-registry-proof-3-title: It ships in a way other people can actually use and trust
norden-registry-proof-intro: The proof is not which brands used the system. The proof is the kind of problem it can actually resolve once it exists.
norden-registry-proof-title: THE PROOF IS A REUSABLE WAY OF SOLVING OPACITY, NOT A CLIENT ROLL CALL.
norden-registry-protocol: Brevo Intelligence Layer // local-first diagnostic and reporting architecture
norden-registry-release-note: Capability dossier // diagnosis, system build, and sovereignty-first handoff
norden-registry-surface-1-copy: Where method, overlap, limits, and decision logic can remain explicit without being flattened for presentation.
norden-registry-surface-2-copy: Where the same truth becomes usable without exposing the whole scaffolding or pretending every audience needs the same depth.
norden-registry-surface-3-copy: Where the system can circulate as an artifact, not just as a live demo that depends on the builder being present.
norden-registry-surface-4-copy: Where the system becomes understandable, operable, and transferable after delivery instead of staying trapped in the builder's head.
norden-registry-surfaces-intro: Good systems do not stop at insight. They have to survive contact with operators, stakeholders, documents, and public-safe contexts without becoming contradictory.
norden-registry-surfaces-title: THE TOOL EARNS TRUST BY STAYING COHERENT ACROSS EVERY HANDOFF SURFACE.
open-to-value: AI Product Engineer & Founding Engineer roles · 100% remote
orchestration-of-digital-projects: Coordinating design, development, content, and stakeholders so projects move without losing the original intent.
pablo-recommendation: Ibai is someone who, once he sets his mind to something (whatever it may be), goes forward and doesn't stop until he becomes a specialist. He seeks to understand the reasons behind things and always goes the extra mile. He is incredibly empathetic, with a high degree of teamwork, making him essential for collaborative work. Technically, he has a very steep learning curve, which helps him acquire new knowledge in ways rarely seen. Without a doubt, if I were to build a development team, it would be a pleasure to have him on my team.
page-description: Founder-operator who codes. Six live systems shipped — RegTech compliance pipelines, multi-tenant SaaS, email infrastructure, IRL discovery, open-source AI tooling. EU citizen, remote, building right now.
page-title: Ibai Fernández — Founder-Operator. AI Product Engineer.
pillar-commercial-sub: I sell well and close what I promise.
pillar-executive-sub: I organize teams and make projects crystallize.
pillar-technical-sub: I code, design, and develop digital products.
pillar-training-sub: Teaching with passion, patience, and judgment.
privacy-s0-p: This site is a personal portfolio. It collects the minimum necessary to function and to understand how visitors arrive. Nothing is sold, nothing is shared with advertisers. Webfonts are served from this domain — no third-party tracking from the moment the page loads.
privacy-s1-h2: 1. Data collected directly by the site
privacy-s1-li1: Contact form submissions. When you submit the contact form, the data you provide (first name, last name, email, subject, message) is sent via the Resend email API to info@ibaifernandez.com. It is not stored on this site or in any database. Resend's privacy policy applies to its handling: resend.com/legal/privacy-policy.
privacy-s1-li2: Anti-spam telemetry. To detect bots, the contact form records a timestamp when the page is loaded and includes it in the submission. It also uses Cloudflare Turnstile, which may load a small token from challenges.cloudflare.com. Turnstile's data handling: developers.cloudflare.com/turnstile.
privacy-s1-li3: Language preference. If you toggle the language, your choice is saved in localStorage on your device under the key portfolio_language. It never leaves your browser.
privacy-s1-li4: Consent decision. If you respond to the cookie banner, your choice is saved in localStorage under the key portfolio_consent. It never leaves your browser.
privacy-s2-accept-li2: Your IP is anonymized before processing (anonymize_ip: true).
privacy-s2-accept-li3: Ad personalization, ad storage, and ad user data remain denied.
privacy-s2-change: Change your mind at any time: use the "Cookie preferences" link in the page footer to re-open the banner and update your choice.
privacy-s2-decline: If you decline (or never respond): GA4 is loaded but never fires. No cookies are set. No data is sent.
privacy-s2-p1: This site uses Google Analytics 4 (property G-T8FTTWBQS3) to understand how visitors arrive and what they read. Analytics is loaded with consent denied by default using Google Consent Mode v2. Nothing is sent to Google until you accept analytics via the consent banner.
privacy-s3-fonts: Webfonts (Roboto, Inter, Josefin Sans, Roboto Mono) are self-hosted from this domain. Google Fonts is not used. Individual dossier pages may load page-specific webfonts from their providers — disclosed at the top of each dossier page when applicable.
privacy-s3-li1: Netlify — hosting CDN and the contact serverless function. Standard server logs (IP, user-agent, requested path) are retained per Netlify's policy: netlify.com/privacy.
privacy-s3-li2: Resend — email delivery for contact form messages.
privacy-s3-li3: Cloudflare Turnstile — bot detection on the contact form.
privacy-s3-li4: Google Analytics 4 — see section 2. Loaded only after explicit consent.
privacy-s3-li5: YouTube — embedded only on the Elm St dossier page, in privacy-enhanced mode (youtube-nocookie.com). YouTube cookies fire only after you press play.
privacy-s4-dsr-h3: How to exercise your rights (Data Subject Request)
privacy-s4-dsr-p: Email info@ibaifernandez.com with subject "Data Subject Request" and describe what you want (access, correction, deletion, etc.). I will respond within 30 calendar days. No proof of identity is required for simple deletion requests; access/portability requests may ask you to confirm the email address used in the original submission.
privacy-s4-intro: Depending on where you reside, you may have rights under one or more of the following frameworks:
privacy-s4-li1: European Union / EEA / UK — General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and UK GDPR.
privacy-s4-li2: Chile — Ley 21.719 sobre Protección de Datos Personales.
privacy-s4-li3: Brasil — Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD, Lei nº 13.709/2018).
privacy-s4-li4: México — Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares (LFPDPPP).
privacy-s4-li5: Other jurisdictions — equivalent local data-protection law may apply.
privacy-s4-rights-intro: Under these frameworks you generally have the right to:
privacy-s4-rights-li1: Know what data is processed about you (this page).
privacy-s4-rights-li2: Request access to, correction of, or deletion of your contact form submissions.
privacy-s4-rights-li3: Withdraw consent for analytics at any time — use the "Cookie preferences" link in the footer.
privacy-s4-rights-li6: Lodge a complaint with your national data-protection authority.
privacy-s5-p: The data controller is Ibai Fernández, the site owner. Contact via info@ibaifernandez.com. There is no joint controller and no designated data protection officer (DPO) — the site does not meet the processing thresholds that would require one under GDPR Art. 37 or equivalent.
privacy-s6-p: Some processors (Resend, Netlify, Cloudflare, Google) are headquartered in the United States and may process data outside your country of residence. Where required, these providers rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or equivalent transfer mechanisms. By using the contact form or accepting analytics, you acknowledge these transfers.
privacy-s7-p: Material changes will be reflected in the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. The full version history is in this repository's git log.
producing-diverse-content: Spearheaded the '2x2MKT' remote initiative during the global pandemic, designing high-volume training funnels that successfully upskilled 4,000+ individuals in digital marketing strategies.
project-aglaya-copy: The case behind a remote digital agency: offer design, client operations, growth experiments, and the systems used to run it.
project-debtracker-copy: A full-stack app for shared debt control, relational data handling, and clearer payment workflows.
project-elmst-copy: A custom-coded brand site that brings reel-first storytelling, audiovisual identity, and a more deliberate web presence into one system.
project-enterprise-crm-copy: Rebuilt agency delivery into a governed operating layer across 18+ enterprise accounts, with POCURO and Leben proving the model under real revenue pressure.
project-gymtracker-copy: A backend-focused training build used to show API design, data modeling, and the transition from marketing into software delivery.
project-myboard-copy: A modular Kanban application built with React, Express, and JSON persistence to prove that task management can be fast, expressive, and fully owned by the operator from day one.
project-national-scale-copy: Led a national-scale digital literacy tour and re-engineered it into a crisis-proof remote operation during the pandemic, onboarding more than 10,600 users into digital conversion funnels.
project-norden-copy: A local-first Brevo intelligence layer built to diagnose opaque operations, structure them into readable decision surfaces, and hand them off without operator dependence.
project-portfolio-copy: Rebuilt this portfolio from a static brochure into a production-validated delivery system: Netlify migration, CI/CD, Playwright coverage, accessibility hardening, media pipelines, and a stable real contact flow.
project-portfolio-title: Portfolio Ibai Fernandez: Live Delivery System
project-research-engine-copy: Turned a raw prompt corpus into a sellable digital product with workflow structure, offer tiers, VSL framing, and live checkout.
project-research-engine-title: The Research Engine: Product Packaging & Launch
sol-recommendation: I had the pleasure of sharing the 4Geeks Bootcamp with him and found him to be an outstanding professional with extensive technical knowledge. He is also eager to learn new things. As a person, it has been a delight to spend this time with him, as he is always willing to help his peers and create a pleasant team atmosphere.
strategic-advisory-on-innovative-digital: Reviewing current surfaces, bottlenecks, and operating gaps to define what should be improved, rebuilt, or productized next.
subject: Subject
successfully-completed: Formalized the engineering toolkit behind the later full-stack and automation work: React, Python, Node.js, APIs, and production-oriented builds.
training-desc-pmi-acp: PMI (PMI-ACP) certification focused on applying agile methodologies — Scrum, Kanban, and Lean — for effective project management.
words-that-warm-the-heart: What people say after working with me
project-scanner-copy: RegTech product in production for preparatory public surface audit under Ley 21.719. Rules first, LLM after. v0.1.1, 273 tests, zero security debt.
